title: Core Data Engineering Concepts
description: Data engineering focuses on building reliable pipelines that move, transform and prepare data for analysis.
trivia: Around 90% of the world's data has been created within the last few years, driven by smartphones, sensors, social media and online services.

title: Databases and Warehouses
description: Databases are used to store structured data, while data warehouses are optimized for analytical queries and reporting.
trivia: The first version of Oracle was released in 1979. It was one of the first commercial relational database management systems.

title: Python and Analytics Libraries
description: Python is widely used in data engineering for its simplicity and rich ecosystem of libraries.
trivia: Python was named after Monty Python's Flying Circus, not the snake.

title: Cloud Computing Platforms
description: Cloud computing platforms provide scalable and on-demand computing resources over the internet.
trivia: Amazon Web Services launched in 2006 with just a handful of services.

title: Data Formats and Storage
description: Data can be stored in various formats, each with its own advantages for storage efficiency and query performance.
trivia: A Parquet file can often be significantly smaller than the same data stored as CSV.

title: DevOps and Deployment
description: DevOps practices aim to streamline the software development and deployment process through automation and collaboration.
trivia: The term 'DevOps' was popularised in 2009 by Patrick Debois.

title: Data Quality and Governance
description: Data quality and governance ensure that data is accurate, consistent, and compliant with organizational policies.
trivia: Bad data quality costs businesses billions of dollars every year.

title: Workflow Orchestration
description: Workflow orchestration involves managing and automating complex workflows across multiple systems and services.
trivia: Apache Airflow was originally developed at Airbnb.

title: Data Modelling
description: Data modelling is the process of creating a conceptual representation of data and its relationships.
trivia: Ralph Kimball and Bill Inmon helped define many modern data warehousing practices.

title: Streaming and Event Processing
description: Streaming and event processing involve handling and analyzing data in real-time as it is generated.
trivia: Apache Kafka was originally developed at LinkedIn and later became an open-source project.

title: SQL and Query Logic
description: SQL (Structured Query Language) is used to manage and manipulate relational databases.
trivia: SQL was originally developed by IBM researchers in the 1970s.

title: Big Data Processing
description: Big data processing involves handling and analyzing large volumes of data that cannot be managed with traditional data processing tools.
trivia: Hadoop was inspired by papers published by Google.

title: Data Lakes
description: Data lakes are centralized repositories that allow you to store all your structured and unstructured data at any scale.
trivia: The term 'data lake' was coined by James Dixon in 2010.

title: Analytics Engineering
description: Analytics engineering involves building and maintaining the infrastructure and processes needed to support data analysis and reporting.
trivia: Analytics engineering is a relatively new role that bridges the gap between data engineering and data analysis.

title: APIs and Data Exchange
description: APIs (Application Programming Interfaces) enable different software systems to communicate and exchange data.
trivia: The first web API was introduced by Salesforce in 2000.

title: Security and Access
description: Security and access control are critical in data engineering to protect sensitive data and ensure that only authorized users can access it.
trivia: The strongest encryption algorithms would take modern computers an impractically long time to brute force.

title: Monitoring and Logging
description: Monitoring and logging involve tracking and recording system activities to ensure proper operation and facilitate troubleshooting.
trivia: The Mars Climate Orbiter was lost because of a units mismatch between systems.

title: Testing Data Pipelines
description: Testing data pipelines involves verifying that data flows correctly through each stage of the pipeline and that the output meets the expected quality standards.
trivia: NASA software projects often devote more effort to testing than coding.

title: Batch Processing
description: Batch processing involves executing a series of jobs in a queued manner without manual intervention.
trivia: Batch processing has been around since the earliest mainframe computers.

title: Data Architecture
description: Data architecture is the design and implementation of the overall structure and organization of an organization's data assets.
trivia: Some enterprise data architectures contain thousands of interconnected systems.

title: Cloud Data Services
description: Cloud data services provide managed solutions for storing, processing, and analyzing data in the cloud.
trivia: Google processes billions of search queries every day.

title: Performance Tuning
description: Performance tuning involves optimizing database and application performance to improve response times and throughput.
trivia: A well-designed index can turn a query from minutes into milliseconds.

title: File Handling
description: File handling involves reading, writing, and managing files in various formats for data processing and storage.
trivia: CSV was never formally standardised for much of its history.

title: Business Intelligence
description: Business intelligence involves the strategies and technologies used to gather, analyze, and present business data for decision-making.
trivia: The first spreadsheet program appeared decades before modern BI tools.

title: Data Careers
description: Data careers encompass a wide range of roles and specializations in the field of data engineering, analytics, and science.
trivia: Demand for data engineers has grown dramatically over the last decade.
